supplemental_discrepancy.consequence: Do NOT classify workload_only or any workload-inclusive variant as historically promotion-admissible. Any V2/V2.1 variant that includes the pitcher OR workload domain is historically INADMISSIBLE for a promotion claim.
supplemental_discrepancy.finding: The starter-identity audit applies not only to the pitcher domain but to EVERY starter-workload feature derived from the selected starter identity. Because historical rows use POSTGAME_ACTUAL_STARTER_ATTRIBUTION and cutoff-known probable starters are unavailable for all 22761 committed games, workload features are postgame-actual-starter dependent.
supplemental_discrepancy.remedy: A new contract version, NRFI_CORE_V2_2_ADMISSIBLE, restricts the historical promotion core to starter-INDEPENDENT domains only: strict-prior team first-inning context, effective-dated park context, and team-level schedule/travel context. Pitcher, workload, and actual-starter identity are excluded from the historical promotion contract and may remain research diagnostics only.
